issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: Did what you asked — ran the ENTIRE release onboarding end-to-end (always-custom path, automated walk). Findings: (1) biological_sex gate works (Continue disabled before a sex is picked, enabled after); (2) workout build ~4s, fine; (3) DIET build = 44-58s = your 'waiting here for too long'. Root cause: the coach 'build it all' fires its meal slots CONCURRENTLY, but the /ai/suggest-meal LLM endpoint throttles concurrent calls to ~the slowest single call, so all meals apply BUNCHED at the very end — the macro bars sat at 0/target and the button label froze on 'Building your meals...' the whole ~44s = reads as hung. (The count:20->8 tuning already landed; the duration is backend-throttle-bound.) FIX shipped (commit 9956a668, pushed to main): the blurred build screen now cycles a staged progress label — 'Reading your goals...' -> 'Picking your meals...' -> 'Balancing your macros...' -> 'Plating it up...' -> 'Finishing your plan...' (7s/stage) — so the wait visibly progresses instead of looking frozen. Verified by re-walking onboarding: at ~9s it shows 'Picking your meals...', at ~30s 'Finishing your plan...', the build completes (macros fill), 0 console errors. OPEN QUESTION for you: this fixes the FELT wait, not the 44s itself. The real speedup (prefetch-warm reliability + the endpoint's concurrency throttle) is a deeper backend effort — want me to take that on next, or is the perceived-progress fix enough for now? (I can't click Resolve myself — the board testGate requires a green full 'npm test', infra-blocked headless. Please Resolve if the progress fix suffices, or direct me on the backend speedup.)
issues.reviewReply: run the entire onboarding, where are the screenshots

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Frontend Sentry crash reporting is DISABLED in production. .env.production has VITE_SENTRY_DSN= (blank); Vite inlines it as an empty string at build time, so initSentry() (src/lib/sentry.ts:26) hits `if(!dsn) return` and the WHOLE Sentry init -- including the `nyus-frontend@<version>` release tag -- is tree-shaken out. Verified on the v150 dist just shipped: no Sentry init, no release literal anywhere. Net: React render crashes / FE runtime errors are NOT reported in production, and the release-tagging work (commit 2dba89c6) has zero effect. .env.example says prod builds should always set a DSN, so this looks like an unfilled-credential oversight, not intent.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner replied 'will add sentry later'. Acknowledged — Sentry DSN intentionally left blank for now. VITE_SENTRY_DSN stays unset in .env.production; frontend crash reporting via Discord errors channel only. No action needed until owner adds DSN. Safe to Resolve (or leave open as a reminder — your call).

issues.description: Extract each question from the onboarding and below that give me deails how it is used for creating the plan which consist of activity-workout-diet how that question effect the plan gen from the coach and create a example plan make sure this is 100% accurate
issues.author: shakeebshaan
issues.reviewReason: DONE — tap 'Open the onboarding->plan map' below for the full report (also live at https://shakeebshaan.github.io/nyus-qa-issues/reports/onboarding-to-plan-map.html). It extracts ALL 20 questions the condensed onboarding asks (across You&Goal / Training / Health&Food), and under each shows its concrete effect on your diet / workout / activity / calorie plan — colour-coded by domain, with the exact frontend+backend file:line for every claim. Then the 3-stage engine (Energy -> Macros -> Content) and a fully worked example: female/29/164cm/78->72kg/fat-loss/fit tier -> BMR 1499 -> TDEE 2323 -> target 1858 kcal -> 195P/142C/57F -> 4-meal split + Upper/Lower x4 workout (3x12-15, RPE7) + step ramp 5k->10k + a sample non-veg lactose-free day. Accuracy: I traced it with 3 parallel code agents and then re-verified the load-bearing numbers myself against live source — Mifflin-St Jeor coefficients (+5 male / -161 female), activity multipliers (1.2-1.725), the 4 intensity-tier specs, and the per-goal rep schemes all match exactly. One honest call-out is in the report: at 1858 kcal the high fat-loss protein target (2.5 g/kg) pushes carbs just under the engine's carb range, which it flags as a soft note (intentional, to spare muscle). I can't click Resolve myself (board testGate needs a green full 'npm test', infra-blocked headless). Please Resolve if this covers it, or tell me what to expand.
issues.reportLabel: Open the onboarding to plan map
issues.reviewReply: What bugs,issues, error do you find and what improvement you suggest use 100 voices for creating plan

issues.description: [Claude-raised] Systemic WCAG-AA contrast gap: white button text (text-primary-foreground) on the domain -500 shades fails 4.5:1. Canvas-resolved sRGB ratios: purple-500 3.84, orange-500 3.14, teal-500 3.05, pink-500 3.79 — all FAIL normal-text AA. The 26-route axe scan only caught /nutrition; the rest render conditionally (coach-chat interactive blocks: ActivityLoggerBlock, CoachChallengeCard, WorkoutTrackerBlock, WellnessCheckinBlock) or use large/bold text (3:1-exempt). I have already fixed the 3 reachable normal-text CTAs: /nutrition empty + error (commit 2475b7b6) and coach-chat error (cc362637), all -500 -> -600. REMAINING decision is owner/design-level because -600 is NOT a blanket fix: teal-600 = 4.48 (still <4.5, needs -700) and orange-600 = 4.50 (borderline). Three options: (A) darken the domain -500 tokens globally in src/index.css so existing classes pass (one change, but shifts the calm/premium domain palette app-wide); (B) keep tokens, enforce a rule "white text uses -700 for teal/orange, -600 for purple/pink" and bump the ~5 remaining coach-chat block CTAs; (C) leave the conditional chat-block CTAs (lowest-traffic) and accept the gap. Recommend (B) — preserves the palette, fixes contrast where text actually sits on the color. Flagging rather than silently darkening ~8 components since it touches the brand domain-color aesthetic (your call).
issues.author: claude

fix.description: Root cause: white text on domain -500 backgrounds failed WCAG AA (orange 3.14, teal 3.05, purple 3.84, pink 3.79). Fix: 20 CTAs across 12 files bumped to passing shades — orange/teal to -700 (6.53/6.66:1), purple/pink to -600 (5.53/5.45:1). Screenshot shows DailyFocus/Home: 'Generate diet plan' (pink-600) and 'Generate workout plan' (orange-700) with correct contrast. Before/after swatch table in attached report. Commit 52bbd660. 121 component tests pass (MealRow, ActivityLogger, CoachChallengeCard, DailyFocus).

judge.note: All 20 CTAs fixed to passing WCAG AA ratios (min 5.45:1), 12 files, 121 tests green, screenshot shows 2 fixed CTAs on the home screen with correct domain-600/700 colors, before/after proof in attached HTML report.

issues.description: [Metrics setup] Connect Google Search Console — unlocks 5 SEO/AEO metrics (search impressions, CTR, avg position, keyword rankings, index coverage), all currently 'awaiting' on the Metrics tab. STEPS: (1) Go to search.google.com/search-console and confirm nyus.in is a verified property (add + verify via DNS TXT if not). (2) Reuse the existing GA4 service account email (the client_email in nyus-ga4-sa.json) OR create a new one: GCP Console -> IAM -> Service Accounts. (3) In GCP Console enable the 'Google Search Console API' for that project. (4) In Search Console -> Settings -> Users and permissions -> Add user -> paste the service-account email -> role 'Full' (or Restricted). (5) Tell me it's done (reply here) and I'll wire scripts that pull searchAnalytics (impressions/clicks/CTR/position by query+page) into the collector. No code needed from you — just the access grant.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner action: grant the service account access to Search Console for nyus.in (5 SEO metrics blocked). Steps in the description.

issues.description: [Metrics setup] Connect billing (Razorpay/Stripe) — unlocks true ARPU, LTV, CAC and accurate MRR. Today the Metrics tab shows REAL subscription counts + gross active revenue from the DB (118 active, ~Rs 10,693), but ARPU/LTV/CAC need the payment provider's history to normalize monthly vs annual vs one-time. STEPS: (1) Razorpay Dashboard -> Settings -> API Keys -> generate a Key ID + Secret (read-only if available). (2) Stripe (if used) -> Developers -> API keys -> a restricted read-only key (read: charges, subscriptions, customers). (3) Send me both via a secure channel (NOT in this public board — drop them in the prod .env or a private note). (4) I'll add a nightly pull computing MRR (normalized), ARPU = MRR/active, churn from cancellations, and LTV = ARPU x avg lifetime. Marketing spend (for CAC) comes from the paid-ads issue.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner action: provide read-only Razorpay/Stripe API keys (securely, not on this public board) to unlock ARPU/LTV/CAC + normalized MRR.

issues.description: [Metrics setup] App-store reporting access — unlocks installs, store conversion (impressions->installs), and store reviews/ratings on the Metrics tab. We already have the Play service account (fastlane-deploy@nyus-and.iam.gserviceaccount.com) used for releases, but it needs reporting + reviews scopes. STEPS: (1) Play Console -> Setup -> API access -> find that service account -> grant 'View app information and download bulk reports' + 'Reply to reviews'. (2) Play Console -> Download reports -> Statistics: confirm the linked Google Cloud Storage bucket exists (installs/CVR live there as CSV). (3) (iOS, later) App Store Connect -> Users and Access -> Keys -> create an App Store Connect API key (Admin/Finance) and send me the .p8 + key id + issuer id securely. (4) Reply here when Play access is granted and I'll wire the Play Developer Reporting API (installs, store-listing CVR) + reviews.list (rating + recent reviews) into the collector.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner action: grant the existing Play service account reporting + reviews access (and optionally create an App Store Connect API key) for installs/CVR/reviews.

issues.description: [Metrics setup] Vulnerability scanning — unlocks 'open high-severity vulnerabilities' (Security, priority H). DECISION FOR YOU: (A) approve the free npm-audit/pip-audit route (I implement it next tick, no action from you), or (B) you prefer Snyk — then create a Snyk account, get a token (Account Settings -> API token), and send it securely. issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner replied 'A' confirming the free npm-audit option. Already implemented last tick — npm audit is wired into the metrics collector, showing 'Open high-severity vulnerabilities = 6 (6 high, 0 critical)' LIVE on the Metrics tab. No further action needed. Safe to Resolve.

issues.description: [Metrics setup] Remaining lower-priority sources (grouped). Each is 'awaiting' on the Metrics tab; tackle as you like: (1) PAID ADS (CPI/CAC/ROAS) — only if you run Google/Meta ads: connect the ad account to GA4 + share read access; until you advertise, leave as N/A. (2) HEALTH-DATA ACCURACY (HR % error, calorie % error) — needs a reference dataset (a chest-strap HR vs app comparison, or indirect-calorimetry numbers); send any calibration data and I'll compute error %. (3) SUPPORT CSAT/NPS + resolution time — either adopt a tool (Zendesk/Intercom) or approve me adding a 1-tap in-app CSAT survey + resolution timestamps to the existing support tables (reply 'add in-app CSAT'). (4) COMPETITOR downloads/revenue — needs a paid Sensor Tower/data.ai seat; or I can do a manual quarterly estimate (reply 'manual'). (5) BACKLINKS — Ahrefs/Moz API token, or I read the GSC 'Links' report once GSC is connected (see the GSC issue). (6) A/B EXPERIMENTS — approve me wiring the existing user_experiment_assignments table into the dashboard (reply 'wire experiments' — likely free, no new tool). Reply with which of these you want and I'll implement/instruct accordingly.
issues.author: claude
issues.reviewReason: Owner said 'Add most of what you can do and I will provide the remaining setups give me steps.' Already live (no creds): A/B experiments, GDPR requests, in-app rating, npm vuln count, AI eval. What I can build now (no creds): IN-APP CSAT 1-tap survey — will build next tick if you say 'build csat'. Steps for what needs YOU: (1) PAID ADS CPI/CAC/ROAS: Google Ads → Link to GA4 in Ads dashboard (Tools > Linked accounts > Google Analytics). Or Meta Ads → set UTMs on campaigns; I pull from GA4 channel breakdown (already reading). No API key needed if you link to GA4. (2) HEALTH ACCURACY: send any reference HR or calorie data (e.g. chest-strap HR log vs app during a workout) — I'll compute % error in a script. Email the CSV to shaan.s@chartraiders.com or paste it in a board reply. (3) COMPETITOR data: Sensor Tower (paid) OR reply 'manual competitor' and I'll research public Play Store listing stats (downloads badge category, ratings count) for 3 top competitors and give a best-estimate table. (4) BACKLINKS: once mt-gsc is connected (GSC service account grant), I pull from GSC Links report automatically — no separate action. Reply 'build csat' or 'manual competitor' to unblock those two.
